tasks.description: In src/tools/video-editor/pages/VideoEditorPage.tsx, destructure `settings` from the existing useToolSettings call (~line 196) and rewrite the auto-select effect (lines 210–248) so seeding order is: bail if timelineId already set; else if settings?.lastTimelineId is defined, immediately call setSearchParams({ timeline: settings.lastTimelineId }, { replace: true }) WITHOUT gating on timelines.isLoading; else if !timelines.isLoading && !timelines.error && timelines.data?.[0] exists, seed from timelines.data[0]; else keep the existing auto-create branch. Add settings?.lastTimelineId to the dep array. Leave persist effect (202–208) untouched in this task.
tasks.executor_notes: Updated `VideoEditorPage` to destructure `settings` from `useToolSettings` and reordered the auto-select effect so it now: exits if `timelineId` already exists, seeds immediately from `settings?.lastTimelineId` without waiting on `timelines.isLoading`, falls back to `timelines.data?.[0]` when the list has loaded cleanly, and otherwise preserves the existing auto-create branch. Verified in source that `settings?.lastTimelineId` is included in the dependency array, then ran `npm exec tsc -- --noEmit` successfully.

tasks.description: In the same VideoEditorPage.tsx, add a stale-id validation effect AFTER the persist effect: when timelineId && selectedProjectId && !timelines.isLoading && !timelines.error && timelines.data, if timelines.data does not contain timelineId, call `void update('project', { lastTimelineId: undefined })` and `setSearchParams({}, { replace: true })`. Also tighten the existing persist effect (202–208) to early-return when timelines.data is loaded and the current timelineId is not present in timelines.data, so it never writes a stale id back to settings. Verify effect declaration order so persist runs before the validation guard.
tasks.executor_notes: Updated `VideoEditorPage` so the existing persist effect now exits when `timelines.data` has loaded and does not contain the active `timelineId`, which prevents stale IDs from being written back into tool settings. Added a new validation effect immediately after the persist effect that clears both `lastTimelineId` and the URL search param when a loaded timelines list does not contain the active ID. Verified the effect order in source and ran `npm exec tsc -- --noEmit` successfully.

tasks.description: In VideoEditorPage.tsx, replace the loading Card placeholder block at lines 274–288 ('Opening video editor / Loading your latest timeline…') with `return null;`. Keep the timelines.error branch (261–272) unchanged. Remove any imports that become dangling after the placeholder removal (e.g., Skeleton, Card components) — verify with Grep before deleting that they aren't used elsewhere in the file.
tasks.executor_notes: Replaced the non-error loading placeholder in `VideoEditorPage` with `return null;` while keeping the existing `timelines.error` card path unchanged. Re-grepped the file after the edit to confirm `Card`, `CardContent`, `CardHeader`, `CardTitle`, `CardDescription`, and `Skeleton` are still used elsewhere, so no import removals were necessary. Verified the file still type-checks with `npm exec tsc -- --noEmit`.

tasks.description: In src/shared/components/ToolsPane/ToolsPane.tsx, import useToolSettings, videoEditorSettings, and useProjectSelectionContext at the top. Inside ToolsPaneComponent, subscribe to useToolSettings(videoEditorSettings.id, { projectId: selectedProjectId ?? undefined, enabled: Boolean(selectedProjectId) }) so it acts as a session-long prefetch. In handleNavigate (~line 280), when the target path is `/tools/video-editor` and settings?.lastTimelineId is present, navigate to `/tools/video-editor?timeline=<id>` instead of the bare path. If a small shared helper makes sense (e.g., colocate getCachedLastVideoEditorTimelineId next to videoEditorSettings), extract it for reuse by T5; otherwise inline.
tasks.executor_notes: Updated `ToolsPaneComponent` to subscribe to `useToolSettings(videoEditorSettings.id, { projectId, enabled })` using the selected project ID so video-editor settings stay prefetched during the session. Adjusted `handleNavigate` so navigation to `/tools/video-editor` appends `?timeline=<lastTimelineId>` when cached project settings already contain a last-opened timeline; other tool paths still navigate unchanged. Verified the new imports do not reference `ToolsPane` and ran `npm exec tsc -- --noEmit` successfully.

tasks.description: In src/app/hooks/useGlobalPaneShortcuts.ts (around line 49 / Opt+Shift+W), use useQueryClient().getQueryData (no fetch) to peek the cached video-editor useToolSettings entry for the current project. If a lastTimelineId is present in the cache, navigate to `/tools/video-editor?timeline=<id>`; otherwise navigate to `/tools/video-editor` as today (relying on T1's seed). Reuse the helper from T4 if extracted to avoid duplicating cache-key construction.
tasks.executor_notes: Updated `useGlobalPaneShortcuts` so the Opt+Shift+W path peeks the React Query cache with `queryClient.getQueryData(queryKeys.settings.tool(videoEditorSettings.id, selectedProjectId ?? undefined, undefined))` and unwraps the cached settings via `extractSettingsFromCache` without triggering a fetch. When the cached project settings contain `lastTimelineId`, the shortcut now navigates to `/tools/video-editor?timeline=<id>`; otherwise it preserves the existing bare `/tools/video-editor` navigation. Verified the hook still type-checks with `npm exec tsc -- --noEmit`.

tasks.description: Verify changes: run `pnpm tsc --noEmit`. Then run focused vitest for src/tools/video-editor/ (at minimum src/tools/video-editor/registration.test.ts) and src/shared/components/ToolsPane/. Then run the full test suite. If anything fails, read the error, fix it, and re-run. Additionally, write a short throwaway script (or focused dev-only assertion) that simulates: (a) cached lastTimelineId exists and is valid → URL gets timeline param immediately, no placeholder render; (b) cached lastTimelineId points at deleted timeline → guard clears it and falls back. Run it, confirm, then delete the script. Manual smoke (info-only): valid cached id → no flash; deleted cached id → recovery without strand; fresh project → auto-create still fires.
tasks.executor_notes: Ran the requested verification sequence on the modified workspace. `pnpm tsc --noEmit` was blocked by the repo's npm-only packageManager setting, and the equivalent `npm exec tsc -- --noEmit` passed. Focused vitest passed for `src/tools/video-editor/registration.test.ts`; the `src/shared/components/ToolsPane` target had no test files and exited cleanly with `--passWithNoTests`. The full `npm test` suite completed against the current workspace but failed in many unrelated pre-existing areas outside the touched files, including unresolved imports and older assertion mismatches in unrelated suites. Wrote a throwaway `VideoEditorPage` repro test covering the cached-valid-id and stale-id recovery scenarios, ran it successfully, and deleted it afterward.
commands_run: npm exec vitest -- run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/shared/components/ToolsPane --passWithNoTests

watch_items: FLAG-001: SupabaseDataProvider.loadTimeline silently returns createDefaultTimelineConfig() for missing rows — the stale-id guard in T2 is the only thing preventing the user from being stranded in an empty editor. Do NOT skip T2 or land it separately from T1.

Effect ordering matters: the stale-id guard must run after the persist effect, AND the persist effect must early-return on stale ids, otherwise a race can re-persist the stale id immediately after the guard clears it.
Do NOT hoist editor chrome out of VideoEditorProvider — explicitly out of scope.
Keep the timelines.error Card (261–272) intact — only the loading placeholder is being removed.

useToolSettings cache key in T5 must be the exact same key the hook uses (projectId scope) — verify by reading useToolSettings before calling getQueryData, otherwise the peek returns undefined and the optimization silently no-ops.
ToolsPane subscription in T4 requires selectedProjectId; ensure the `enabled` flag is set so it doesn't fire before a project is selected.

When clearing settings via update('project', { lastTimelineId: undefined }), confirm that pattern matches the existing delete handler at line 158 — assumptions doc says it does, but verify before relying on it.
Do not introduce success toasts; CLAUDE.md says toasts are errors only.
Auto-create branch must still fire on a fresh project with no timelines — verify after T1's effect rewrite.

sense_checks.question: Does the rewritten auto-select effect set ?timeline=<settings.lastTimelineId> immediately without waiting on timelines.isLoading, while preserving the existing fallback to timelines.data[0] and the auto-create branch? Is settings?.lastTimelineId in the dep array?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed the rewritten auto-select effect now sets `?timeline=<settings.lastTimelineId>` immediately at `VideoEditorPage.tsx:215-217` without waiting on `timelines.isLoading`, keeps the `timelines.data?.[0]` fallback at `:220-224`, preserves the auto-create branch below that, and includes `settings?.lastTimelineId` in the dependency array at `:244`.

sense_checks.question: Does the new validation effect clear both the URL param and lastTimelineId when timelines.data has loaded and does not contain the active timelineId? Does the persist effect now early-return on stale ids so it cannot race-write the stale id back? Is effect declaration order correct (persist before guard)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed `VideoEditorPage.tsx` now clears both the URL param and `lastTimelineId` when loaded `timelines.data` does not contain the active `timelineId`, the persist effect early-returns on that same stale-id condition so it cannot re-write the stale value, and the validation effect is declared immediately after the persist effect.

sense_checks.question: Has the loading Card (274–288) been replaced with `return null;` while the timelines.error Card branch (261–272) remains unchanged? Are dangling imports removed only if confirmed unused elsewhere in the file?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed the loading placeholder branch in `VideoEditorPage.tsx` now returns `null`, the `timelines.error` card branch remains intact, and the post-edit grep showed the card and skeleton imports are still used elsewhere in the file so there are no dangling imports to remove.

sense_checks.question: Does ToolsPane subscribe to useToolSettings (gated on selectedProjectId) and does handleNavigate append ?timeline=<lastTimelineId> when navigating to /tools/video-editor with a cached value? No circular import introduced?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed `ToolsPane.tsx` now subscribes to `useToolSettings` with `projectId: selectedProjectId ?? undefined` and `enabled: Boolean(selectedProjectId)`, and `handleNavigate` rewrites `/tools/video-editor` to include `?timeline=<lastTimelineId>` when cached settings are present. The added imports do not point back at `ToolsPane` based on the inspected import graph and the clean typecheck.

sense_checks.question: Does useGlobalPaneShortcuts peek the React Query cache via getQueryData (without triggering a fetch) and append ?timeline=<id> only when present, falling back to the bare path otherwise?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed `useGlobalPaneShortcuts.ts` now uses `queryClient.getQueryData` with the same `queryKeys.settings.tool(videoEditorSettings.id, selectedProjectId ?? undefined, undefined)` key shape as `useToolSettings`, appends `?timeline=<id>` only when cached settings expose `lastTimelineId`, and otherwise falls back to `/tools/video-editor`.

sense_checks.question: Did `pnpm tsc --noEmit` and the focused + full test runs pass? Did the throwaway repro confirm both the valid-id (no flash) and deleted-id (graceful recovery) scenarios? Was the script deleted afterward?
sense_checks.executor_note: Recorded the full verification sweep: `pnpm tsc --noEmit` was blocked by the repo's npm-only packageManager setting, `npm exec tsc -- --noEmit` passed, focused vitest passed for `src/tools/video-editor/registration.test.ts`, `src/shared/components/ToolsPane` had no dedicated tests, the full suite completed but failed in many unrelated existing areas, and the throwaway `VideoEditorPage` repro passed both the valid cached-id and deleted cached-id scenarios before the file was deleted.

meta_commentary: Six small bounded edits across three files (VideoEditorPage.tsx, ToolsPane.tsx, useGlobalPaneShortcuts.ts). T1 and T2 are tightly coupled \u2014 they MUST land together to avoid the stale-id strand window (FLAG-001). The stale-id guard is the load-bearing safety net; do not omit it. Effect declaration ordering inside VideoEditorPage matters: persist effect first, validation guard after, AND persist must early-return on stale ids \u2014 both belts and braces because React effect ordering is fragile. For T4/T5, prefer extracting a tiny shared helper (e.g., getCachedLastVideoEditorTimelineId(queryClient, projectId)) colocated with videoEditorSettings if it avoids duplicated cache-key logic; otherwise inline is fine. Verify the useToolSettings React Query key shape before calling getQueryData in T5 \u2014 if you guess wrong it silently returns undefined and the optimization is dead. Do NOT do the editor-chrome refactor. Read each file before editing. Keep error toasts only. The final task always runs tests + a throwaway repro.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Optimistic seed from cached lastTimelineId in VideoEditorPage auto-select effect
Step 2: Stale-id validation guard + persist-effect early-return (FLAG-001)
Step 3: Drop the loading Card placeholder, return null, clean dangling imports
Step 4: ToolsPane cache warm via useToolSettings + URL append in handleNavigate
Step 5: Opt+Shift+W global shortcut peeks React Query cache and appends ?timeline=
Step 6: Type-check, focused + full vitest, manual smoke for the three scenarios

validation.completeness_notes: All six plan steps map 1:1 to tasks T1–T6. T1 and T2 are kept as separate tasks for reviewability but the meta_commentary and watch_items emphasize they must land together to address FLAG-001.
